message:
fix(ft-api): index_add / index_copy compose through scatter_add / scatter for autograd

Same severed-autograd pattern Scatter / ScatterAdd / IndexPut had
until the recent fixes (5d4b5a1 / 56c5165 / 692d90e / 98ea841):
both tensor_index_add and tensor_index_copy extracted values via
self.tensor_tape.values, computed the scatter in plain f64, and
rebuilt a fresh requires_grad=false leaf — silently dropping the
gradient that should reach BOTH input and src. PyTorch's
torch.Tensor.index_add and .index_copy are fully differentiable.

Strategy:
  index_add  ≡ tensor_scatter_add(input, dim, expanded_idx, src)
  index_copy ≡ tensor_scatter    (input, dim, expanded_idx, src)

Both scatter primitives now correctly back-propagate through input
(passthrough or zero-out-overwritten) and src (gather, with
last-write-wins masking on duplicates for the non-add scatter), so
the composition gives end-to-end autograd for free.

The 1-D `index` argument is "expanded" by hand into a contiguous
[outer, src_dim_size, inner] index tensor where every (o, r, i)
slot holds idx_1d[r] — exactly the shape the underlying
tensor_scatter / tensor_scatter_add expects (matching src). Up-front
index validation is preserved to keep the existing diagnostic
error messages on out-of-range / non-finite indices.

Tests:
  index_add_propagates_gradients_to_input_and_src: input=[1..5],
    indices=[1,3], src=[10,30] → forward [1,12,3,34,5];
    sum loss → grad_input = ones (passthrough), grad_src = [1, 1].
  index_add_duplicate_indices_double_count_src_gradient: indices
    [0, 0] writing src=[10, 20] → both writes contribute to output[0]
    and each src position correctly receives incoming[0] (PyTorch
    accumulate semantics — no de-duplication).
  index_copy_propagates_gradients_to_input_and_src: indices=[1,3]
    overwriting → grad_input = [1, 0, 1, 0] (two slots clobbered),
    grad_src = [1, 1].
  index_copy_duplicate_indices_use_last_writer_for_src_gradient:
    indices [0, 0] writing src=[10, 20] → last write wins,
    grad_src = [0, 1].

All 20 existing forward-only tests continue to pass — bit-for-bit
forward parity preserved because the scatter primitives implement
exactly the same write semantics as the previous in-place loops.

Workspace: 1108 ft-api tests green, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
